text:
```exs astotel/lib/astotel.exs
defmodule Astotel do
  @moduledoc """
  Documentation for `Astotel`.
  """

  @doc """
  Hello world.

  ## Examples

      iex> Astotel.hello()
      :world

  """
  @months_prefix [
    "Jan",
    "Fev",
    "Mar",
    "Avr",
    "Mai",
    "Juin",
    "Jul",
    "Aout",
    "Sep",
    "Oct",
    "Nov",
    "Dec"
  ]

  def convert do
    Path.wildcard("data/*.csv")
    |> Enum.map(fn file ->
      dbg(file)

      {year, _} =
        String.slice(file, 5..8)
        |> Integer.parse()

      output_file = File.stream!("output/Astotel-occupation-#{year}.csv")

      file
      |> File.stream!()
      |> CSV.decode(headers: true, separator: ?;)
      |> Stream.flat_map(fn
        {:ok, row} ->
          parse_row(row, year)

        {:error, error} ->
          IO.puts("Error: #{inspect(error)}")
          nil

        _ ->
          nil
      end)
      |> Stream.map(fn
        [date, hotel, occupation_rate, daily_occupants] ->
          %{
            "DATE" => Date.to_string(date),
            "NOMHOTEL" => hotel,
            "TAUXOCCUPATION" => occupation_rate,
            "NBCLIENTS" => daily_occupants
          }
      end)
      |> CSV.encode(headers: ["DATE", "NOMHOTEL", "TAUXOCCUPATION", "NBCLIENTS"], separator: ?;)
      |> Stream.into(output_file)
      |> Stream.run()
    end)
  end

  defp parse_row(row, year) do
    hotel = row["Hotel"]
    hotel_id = get_hostel_id(hotel)

    1..12
    |> Enum.filter(fn month ->
      month_prefix = @months_prefix |> Enum.at(month - 1)
      Map.get(row, month_prefix <> "TO") != nil
    end)
    |> Enum.flat_map(fn month ->
      month_prefix = @months_prefix |> Enum.at(month - 1)
      {:ok, date_begin} = Date.new(year, month, 1)
      date_end = Date.end_of_month(date_begin)
      nb_days = Date.diff(date_end, date_begin) + 1

      occupation_rate =
        case row[month_prefix <> "TO"] do
          nil ->
            0

          raw_occupation_rate ->
            {occupation_rate, _} =
              raw_occupation_rate
              |> String.replace("%", "")
              |> String.replace(",", ".")
              |> String.trim()
              |> Float.parse()

            occupation_rate
        end

      nb_occupants =
        case row[month_prefix <> "Occupants"] do
          nil ->
            0

          raw_occupants ->
            dbg(month_prefix <> "Occupants, #{raw_occupants}")

            raw_occupants
            |> String.replace(",00", "")
            |> String.to_integer()
        end

      daily_occupants =
        case nb_occupants do
          0 ->
            0

          _ ->
            (nb_occupants / nb_days) |> Float.ceil(0)
        end

      Enum.map(1..nb_days, fn day ->
        {:ok, date} = Date.new(year, month, day)
        [date, hotel_id, occupation_rate, daily_occupants]
      end)
    end)
  end

  defp get_hostel_id(name) do
    %{
      "Hotel ASTRA" => 21910,
      "Hotel ASTORIA" => 21911,
      "Hotel ACADIA" => 21912,
      "Hotel BRADFORD" => 21913,
      "Hotel 123 ELYSEE" => 21914,
      "Hotel MALTE" => 21915,
      "Hotel LORETTE" => 21916,
      "Hotel GEORGE" => 21917,
      "Hotel AUGUSTIN" => 21918,
      "Hotel CAUMARTIN" => 21919,
      "Hotel JOYCE" => 21920,
      "Hotel PALM" => 21921,
      "Hotel MONTEROSA" => 21922,
      "Hotel SEBASTOPOL" => 21923,
      "Hotel REGENT'S GARDEN" => 21924,
      "Hotel 34B" => 21925,
      "Hotel JOKE" => 21926
    }
    |> Map.get(name)
  end
end

Astotel.convert()
```

Float to integer

To convert a float to an integer in Elixir, you can use one of these methods:

1. Using `trunc/1` which truncates the decimal part:
```elixir
daily_occupants = trunc(nb_occupants / nb_days)
```

2. Using `round/1` which rounds to the nearest integer:
```elixir
daily_occupants = round(nb_occupants / nb_days)
```

In your code, you're currently using `Float.ceil(0)` which rounds up to the nearest integer. If you want to simply convert to an integer without rounding up, replace:

```elixir
daily_occupants =
  case nb_occupants do
    0 ->
      0

    _ ->
      (nb_occupants / nb_days) |> Float.ceil(0)
  end
```

with:

```elixir
daily_occupants =
  case nb_occupants do
    0 ->
      0

    _ ->
      (nb_occupants / nb_days) |> round()  # or trunc() depending on your needs
  end
```

Choose between `round/1` or `trunc/1` based on your requirements:
- `round/1` if you want to round to the nearest integer (e.g., 3.6 becomes 4, 3.4 becomes 3)
- `trunc/1` if you want to just remove the decimal part (e.g., both 3.6 and 3.4 become 3)
summary: Convert Float Numbers to Integer Values
